Catapult is building the future of sports performance technology, with a mission to Unleash the Potential of every athlete and team on earth. We don't just work in the sporting industry; we are actively changing it.  Since 2006, our solutions have been leading the way in sports performance software, science, and data, in a world where 1% can literally mean the difference between winning and losing.

We work with over 5,000+ teams around the world, empowering coaches, managers and trainers in premier teams in the NFL, NBA, NHL, MLS, EPL, AFL, NRL, NCAA and more. We provide the information they need to optimize athletes’ health, game-day readiness, and performance, as well as in-game tactics.  

Catapult is a sports technology company that empowers professional teams to make data-driven decisions. We deliver health, performance, video, and AI insights from the locker room to competitive environments, ensuring every decision is an opportunity to gain an advantage, sharpen performance, and build lasting success. 

WE WANT PEOPLE WHO ARE PASSIONATE ABOUT USING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE TO BUILD AMAZING PRODUCTS


We are looking for a Principal AI Engineer who thinks in graphs, builds in Go, and believes the value of an AI system lives in the knowledge structure underneath it, not the language model on top. Based in Melbourne, you will own the core intelligence architecture for Catapult's next-generation platform: connecting the measurements we're known for into a system that reasons about sport. This is a small team with executive sponsorship, building at the intersection of knowledge graphs, LLM reasoning, and elite sports data. If you care more about getting the architecture right than following a process, we should talk.

We are building a system that reasons about sport. Not a chatbot on top of dashboards. Not a retrieval system that finds the right spreadsheet. A sensemaking system that connects all the measurements Catapult is known for into a knowledge graph, and lets anyone from the GM to the sideline coach ask questions in the language of their sport and get evidence-backed answers.

The major cloud and enterprise AI companies have validated this architecture pattern in production. Nobody has built it for sport. We are.

You will own the core intelligence architecture: the Neo4j knowledge graph, the context synthesis pipeline (map-reduce over graph communities), the agent orchestration, and the evaluation framework that keeps it honest. You are the most senior technical person on a small team. There is no technical hierarchy above you. The Head of AI sets direction, not architecture. You make the technical calls.

The stack: Go for the intelligence service, Neo4j for the knowledge graph, AWS Bedrock for model hosting, PromptFoo for eval, Python for data pipelines, React for practitioner tools, Rust for performance-critical paths. You don't need to know all of these on day one. You need to be able to learn any of them in a week.

WHAT YOU’LL DO 

Design and build the knowledge graph architecture in Neo4j. A structured representation of how sports data, practitioner behaviour, and domain concepts relate to each other. Temporal facts, community detection, confidence scoring.
Build the context synthesis pipeline: the system that assembles the right subgraph into the right context for the right query. Map-reduce over graph communities. The quality of the agent's output is limited by the quality of the context you assemble.
Build the intelligence service in Go that turns structured knowledge into practitioner-facing answers. Not text summaries. Rich, evidence-backed responses with provenance, a practitioner can click through.
Build the integration layer that connects Catapult's product data (wearables, gym, video, positioning) into the graph. Multiple sources, different frequencies, different schemas, one connected knowledge layer.
Build evaluation infrastructure from day one. Eval-driven development: define what good looks like before building, iterate until evals pass, monitor with evals in production. PromptFoo for prompt regression. LLM-as-judge for sensemaking quality. Per-step reliability measurement across the pipeline.
Build internal validation tools so domain experts can see, challenge, and correct the system's output. The correction IS the product's most valuable training signal.
Set the technical standard for a team that uses AI coding tools as the primary development environment, not a supplement. Multiple parallel sessions. Written communication over meetings. Async-first.
Sit on the architecture committee. You connect this team to the broader engineering organisation's technical governance.

WHAT YOU’LL NEED

Required (you won't tick every box, we're looking for the person, not the checklist):

You have built systems that use LLMs for reasoning over structured data. At work, on a side project, or at 2am because you couldn't stop. Not chatbots. Not RAG over documents. Systems where an LLM reasons over connected knowledge and produces answers from multiple sources.
You think in data relationships, not just tables. Graph experience is ideal but not required. Strong opinions about how to model connected data, and the curiosity to go deep on graph architectures once you're here.
You can hold a full system architecture in your head, from data ingestion through graph construction through context assembly to user-facing response, and make trade-off decisions without waiting for approval.
You have used AI coding tools seriously enough to have developed your own workflow. Not tried them. Developed a workflow. You have opinions about eval-driven development.
You are comfortable being the most senior technical person in a small team with no tech lead above you.
Strong fundamentals in at least one systems language (Go, Rust, C++) and one scripting language (Python, TypeScript). We care about engineering judgment more than language fluency.

Highly valued (same rule, we're looking for the person, not the checklist):

Experience with Neo4j or other graph databases in production.
Experience with GraphRAG patterns, community detection, or knowledge graph construction from unstructured data.
Experience with real-time data pipelines and temporal alignment of multi-source, multi-frequency data.
Experience building LLM evaluation pipelines (PromptFoo, custom eval frameworks, automated scoring, regression detection).
Experience with AWS Bedrock, model hosting, or multi-model orchestration.
Familiarity with sports data, sensor systems, or wearable technology. Not required, but helpful.

You should not apply if:

You need a tech lead to review your architecture decisions.
You have only worked with LLMs through wrapper libraries and haven't thought about prompt engineering, evaluation, or cost optimisation.
You are looking for a research role. This is applied engineering; shipping products.
You prefer working in a well-defined system with clear tickets and sprint ceremonies. We're running like an R&D team, not a feature shipping team. Our practices are built on hypothesis testing and experiments, not sprints and story points.

WHY CATAPULT? 

We have amazing people. We promise you’ll work with some of the most ambitious,  intelligent people in an exciting industry, and do some of the best work of your life.
We encourage our people to engage in constructive, open, and honest communication to make Catapult extraordinary. 
We work in a collaborative yet challenging environment to consistently improve our performance, which in turn impacts our customers' performance.
Our workforce spans more than 20 countries. You'll have the opportunity to work with people from multiple nationalities and cultures, and to build your global awareness.  
We value improvement and development. We are challenging ourselves to continuously grow and become a high-performance company. That means we maintain a growth mindset in everything we do, and our people are always looking for ways to improve.  There is an unlimited opportunity to grow, do more, and do better.

Whether you’re interested in sports or not, you’ll have the satisfaction of knowing your work is supporting some of the most successful teams and athletes on the planet! 

Research shows that while men apply for jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalized groups tend only to apply when they check every box. So if you have what it takes, but don't meet every single point in our job ad, please still get in touch! We would love to have a chat and see if you could be a great addition to our team. We are building the future of sports performance. Our priority is to find the brightest talent who can add to our team culture, actively contribute, and be excited about what they do.

All offers of employment are subject to Catapult's positive prehire check. To find out more, please contact the Talent Partner for this role.

At Macpherson Kelley, people come first, always. Our clients and team are at the heart of everything we do.

About the Opportunity

We are seeking an AI Analyst to join our IT team on a permanent full-time basis. This is a unique opportunity to apply cutting-edge AI in a real-world, high-impact environment. You will work alongside legal professionals to design, develop, and deploy AI-driven solutions that enhance legal service delivery and operational efficiency across the firm.

What you’ll be doing:

Design and build AI models and applications that solve problems and maximise efficiency.
Develop tools that automate and support legal tasks like research, contract review, due diligence, etc.
Collaborate with lawyers to embed AI tools into day-to-day workflows.
Identify opportunities to improve efficiency across practice areas using AI.
Monitor performance, accuracy and reliability of AI systems.
Stay up to date on emerging AI trends, particularly in legal technology.

This role can be based in either our Melbourne CBD or Dandenong office or split between both locations. 

About You

To be successful in this role, you will have:

Previous experience building AI/machine learning (ML) solutions.
Proficiency in Python and AI/ML frameworks.
Experience in large language models (LLMs) and prompt engineering.
Experience developing solutions using Microsoft’s Power Platform alongside AI.
Experience working in a legal or professional services environment (preferred).
Understanding of legal workflows and document-heavy processes (desirable).
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering or related field.
Postgraduate qualifications or certifications in AI/ML (desirable).

Our Firm

Macpherson Kelley is a national Commercial law firm that thinks differently. Our people possess a rare combination of commercial acumen, legal ingenuity, and human insight. That’s why we treat each client’s business as if it’s our own, delivering intelligent recommendations they can act on.

With offices in Brisbane, Dandenong, Melbourne, Sydney and Adelaide, as well as international connections through our Pacific Legal Network, Multilaw and PrivacyRules partners, our lawyers collaborate across practice areas and borders to take care of any legal issues faced by our clients.

Our People Promise

The best thing about Macpherson Kelley is its people. Really good people and talented lawyers, in an inclusive, high performing and positive workplace culture achieve great things. We are flexible and outcome focused in how we work, so you can get on with the job. Together, we believe in better and are ambitious for growth, and we understand that growth comes from leveraging all business and learning opportunities, many of which are delivered through our practical and commercially focused Macpherson Kelley Business School.

Our benefits include mentoring programs, additional leave options, and generous parental leave policies. Learn more about building your career with us at www.mk.com.au/careers.

This is a high-impact leadership role focused on operational execution, engineering excellence, capability uplift, and scalable AI adoption across a large and complex environment.

The organisation has recently consolidated its Data Engineering and AI/ML Engineering functions into a unified capability and is looking for a technically credible leader who can bring structure, alignment and maturity across platforms, engineering practices and delivery teams.

This is not a pure strategy role. The strategic direction is established; the focus now is on execution, people leadership, technical credibility, and scaling enterprise capability.

The Role
The Senior Director- Data & AI Engineering will lead a large-scale Data & AI Engineering function responsible for enterprise data platforms, AI/ML engineering capability, engineering standards, and AI enablement.

Key Responsibilities
• Lead enterprise Data Engineering and AI/ML Engineering functions across platform, delivery and capability domains
• Lead, develop and retain high-performing engineering and delivery leaders across Data Engineering, ML Engineering and AI Engineering
• Establish and enforce engineering standards, patterns, frameworks and best practices across data and AI
• Drive maturity across modern engineering disciplines including CI/CD, MLOps, automation, observability and infrastructure-as-code
• Partner closely with Architecture, Technology and Business leadership to ensure alignment between design intent and delivery execution
• Lead the transition from fragmented capability toward a more consistent, scalable and governed engineering model
• Embed operational discipline across data and AI platforms, ensuring reliability, scalability and performance
• Support safe, controlled and scalable adoption of AI and GenAI capabilities across the enterprise
• Manage and optimise large-scale vendor and contractor ecosystems to support delivery outcomes and capability uplift
• Foster a culture of accountability, technical excellence and continuous improvement

About You
You are a senior technology and engineering leader with deep technical credibility and proven experience leading large-scale Data, AI or Platform Engineering organisations within complex enterprise environments.
You are equally comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders and working through technical execution detail where required.

You will bring:
• Proven experience leading enterprise-scale Data Engineering, AI/ML Engineering or Platform Engineering organisations
• Strong technical depth across modern data platforms, cloud ecosystems and AI/ML engineering practices
• Demonstrated ability to operate in complex, matrixed enterprise environments with fragmented or evolving operating models
• Experience driving engineering maturity, standardisation and operational excellence across large teams
• Strong leadership capability across both direct and indirect reporting structures
• Hands-on understanding of modern engineering practices including CI/CD, MLOps, automation and observability
• A track record of improving delivery consistency, execution quality and engineering discipline
• The ability to influence senior technology stakeholders while maintaining credibility with engineering teams
• A pragmatic, delivery-oriented leadership style with strong commercial and operational awareness

At HammondCare, data is not just information it is a critical enabler of safe, high‑quality care, trusted decision‑making, and responsible innovation.

The Data Architect plays a pivotal role in shaping how data is designed, governed, and used across HammondCare. This role is accountable for defining and evolving both the current‑state and future‑state enterprise data architecture, ensuring our data is trusted, secure, interoperable, and fit‑for‑purpose across clinical, operational, and corporate domains.

Working closely with clinical leaders, business teams, technology partners, and vendors, the Data Architect establishes clear enterprise data standards, patterns, and models that enable reliable reporting, analytics, AI‑enabled insights, and compliance with regulatory and ethical obligations.

This is a hands‑on, outcomes‑focused role translating strategy into practical architecture decisions that improve data quality, reduce risk, and enable HammondCare to scale responsibly while staying true to our mission of care.

Title: Mechanical Design Engineer

Full Time

About Next Level Racing®

Next Level Racing® is a global leader in simulation hardware, delivering industry-leading solutions for racing and flight simulation enthusiasts worldwide. With products available in over 60 countries, Next Level Racing® has built a strong reputation for innovation, quality, and immersive experiences across consumer and commercial markets.


As an official partner and licensee of leading global brands and organisations including the FIA, Ferrari, Boeing, Airbus, Cessna, Microsoft Flight Simulator, PlayStation®, and iRacing, Next Level Racing® continues to push the boundaries of simulation technology.


Headquartered on the Gold Coast, Australia, Next Level Racing® designs and develops racing cockpits, flight cockpits, motion platforms, haptic technology, seating solutions, and accessories used by gamers, esports competitors, professional drivers, aviation enthusiasts, and simulation centers globally.


At Next Level Racing®, we foster a fast-paced and collaborative environment where innovation, creativity, and passion drive the future of simulation.


Job Description

We are seeking an experienced Mechanical Engineer to join our dynamic Research & Development team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in mechanical engineering and FEA analysis. Experience in steel-fabricated components, plastic components, and mass-manufactured products is desirable. They will have a passion for motorsport or flight and/or gaming, which will aid them in creating the cutting-edge racing simulation products that NLR is so well-known for. This role requires a creative problem-solver who can utilize their engineering expertise to work collaboratively with the design team and develop products from concept to production, ensuring the highest standards of quality and performance.


Key Responsibilities

Product Design and Development: Design and develop racing and flight simulation products from initial concept through to mass-manufacture production. DFMA is crucial to this role.

CAD Modelling and Simulation: Create detailed 3D CAD models and perform simulations to validate designs through reports.

Prototyping and Testing: Develop prototypes and oversee testing to ensure products meet performance and durability standards.

Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams, including marketing, customer service, and external manufacturers, to ensure cohesive product development.

Innovation: Stay updated with industry trends and technological advancements to incorporate new ideas into product designs.

Delivering to deadlines: Manage engineering project timelines to deliver products on schedule and within scope.

Quality Assurance: Ensure all products meet stringent quality standards and regulatory requirements.




Qualifications, Experience, and Attributes

Education: Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field.

Experience: Minimum of 5 years of experience in mechanical engineering, preferably in structural steel or aluminum design. Experience in the simulation, gaming, or automotive industries will be advantageous.

Technical Skills: Proficient in CAD software (e.g. SolidWorks, Rhino), FEA analysis, prototyping tools, and Adobe Suites. Experience with manufacturing processes and materials.

Problem-Solving: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot and resolve complex design issues with creative design thinking.

Creativity: Demonstrated ability to innovate and develop new concepts that enhance user experience and product functionality whilst making efficient use of materials.

Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present ideas clearly and collaborate effectively with team members.

Attention to Detail: High attention to detail and a commitment to producing high-quality work.





What We Offer

Competitive Salary: Commensurate with experience and qualifications.

Growth Opportunities: Opportunities for professional development and career advancement.

Work Environment: We are based in a central Gold Coast location, operating from our world-class HQ building. We are proud of the fun, collaborative and high-energy team culture that permeates through our entire team here at Next Level Racing.

Passion for Racing and Flight: Be part of a team that shares your passion for racing or flight and cutting-edge technology.



What Makes This Role Different

You'll be contributing to the shaping of a global brand, designing products used by thousands of people world-wide. We have built and continue to build a creative team that values collaboration, ownership, and positive energy; where great ideas are encouraged, autonomy is expected, and your work directly impacts how thousands of enthusiasts experience simulation.

The reality: This is a fast-moving environment. Priorities shift. Deadlines are tight. You'll need to be comfortable with ambiguity and thrive under pressure. If you love the buzz of a dynamic creative workflow and want to make an impact, you'll fit right in.

Catapult is building the future of sports performance technology, with a mission to Unleash the Potential of every athlete and team on earth. We don't just work in the sporting industry; we are actively changing it.  Since 2006, our solutions have been leading the way in sports performance software, science, and data, in a world where 1% can literally mean the difference between winning and losing.

We work with over 5,000+ teams around the world, empowering coaches, managers and trainers in premier teams in the NFL, NBA, NHL, MLS, EPL, AFL, NRL, NCAA and more. We provide the information they need to optimize athletes’ health, game-day readiness, and performance, as well as in-game tactics.  

Catapult is a sports technology company that empowers professional teams to make data-driven decisions. We deliver health, performance, video, and AI insights from the locker room to competitive environments, ensuring every decision is an opportunity to gain an advantage, sharpen performance, and build lasting success. 

WE WANT PEOPLE WHO ARE PASSIONATE ABOUT USING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE TO BUILD AMAZING PRODUCTS


We are looking for a Principal AI Engineer who thinks in graphs, builds in Go, and believes the value of an AI system lives in the knowledge structure underneath it, not the language model on top. Based in Melbourne, you will own the core intelligence architecture for Catapult's next-generation platform: connecting the measurements we're known for into a system that reasons about sport. This is a small team with executive sponsorship, building at the intersection of knowledge graphs, LLM reasoning, and elite sports data. If you care more about getting the architecture right than following a process, we should talk.

We are building a system that reasons about sport. Not a chatbot on top of dashboards. Not a retrieval system that finds the right spreadsheet. A sensemaking system that connects all the measurements Catapult is known for into a knowledge graph, and lets anyone from the GM to the sideline coach ask questions in the language of their sport and get evidence-backed answers.

The major cloud and enterprise AI companies have validated this architecture pattern in production. Nobody has built it for sport. We are.

You will own the core intelligence architecture: the Neo4j knowledge graph, the context synthesis pipeline (map-reduce over graph communities), the agent orchestration, and the evaluation framework that keeps it honest. You are the most senior technical person on a small team. There is no technical hierarchy above you. The Head of AI sets direction, not architecture. You make the technical calls.

The stack: Go for the intelligence service, Neo4j for the knowledge graph, AWS Bedrock for model hosting, PromptFoo for eval, Python for data pipelines, React for practitioner tools, Rust for performance-critical paths. You don't need to know all of these on day one. You need to be able to learn any of them in a week.

WHAT YOU’LL DO 

Design and build the knowledge graph architecture in Neo4j. A structured representation of how sports data, practitioner behaviour, and domain concepts relate to each other. Temporal facts, community detection, confidence scoring.
Build the context synthesis pipeline: the system that assembles the right subgraph into the right context for the right query. Map-reduce over graph communities. The quality of the agent's output is limited by the quality of the context you assemble.
Build the intelligence service in Go that turns structured knowledge into practitioner-facing answers. Not text summaries. Rich, evidence-backed responses with provenance, a practitioner can click through.
Build the integration layer that connects Catapult's product data (wearables, gym, video, positioning) into the graph. Multiple sources, different frequencies, different schemas, one connected knowledge layer.
Build evaluation infrastructure from day one. Eval-driven development: define what good looks like before building, iterate until evals pass, monitor with evals in production. PromptFoo for prompt regression. LLM-as-judge for sensemaking quality. Per-step reliability measurement across the pipeline.
Build internal validation tools so domain experts can see, challenge, and correct the system's output. The correction IS the product's most valuable training signal.
Set the technical standard for a team that uses AI coding tools as the primary development environment, not a supplement. Multiple parallel sessions. Written communication over meetings. Async-first.
Sit on the architecture committee. You connect this team to the broader engineering organisation's technical governance.

WHAT YOU’LL NEED

Required (you won't tick every box, we're looking for the person, not the checklist):

You have built systems that use LLMs for reasoning over structured data. At work, on a side project, or at 2am because you couldn't stop. Not chatbots. Not RAG over documents. Systems where an LLM reasons over connected knowledge and produces answers from multiple sources.
You think in data relationships, not just tables. Graph experience is ideal but not required. Strong opinions about how to model connected data, and the curiosity to go deep on graph architectures once you're here.
You can hold a full system architecture in your head, from data ingestion through graph construction through context assembly to user-facing response, and make trade-off decisions without waiting for approval.
You have used AI coding tools seriously enough to have developed your own workflow. Not tried them. Developed a workflow. You have opinions about eval-driven development.
You are comfortable being the most senior technical person in a small team with no tech lead above you.
Strong fundamentals in at least one systems language (Go, Rust, C++) and one scripting language (Python, TypeScript). We care about engineering judgment more than language fluency.

Highly valued (same rule, we're looking for the person, not the checklist):

Experience with Neo4j or other graph databases in production.
Experience with GraphRAG patterns, community detection, or knowledge graph construction from unstructured data.
Experience with real-time data pipelines and temporal alignment of multi-source, multi-frequency data.
Experience building LLM evaluation pipelines (PromptFoo, custom eval frameworks, automated scoring, regression detection).
Experience with AWS Bedrock, model hosting, or multi-model orchestration.
Familiarity with sports data, sensor systems, or wearable technology. Not required, but helpful.

You should not apply if:

You need a tech lead to review your architecture decisions.
You have only worked with LLMs through wrapper libraries and haven't thought about prompt engineering, evaluation, or cost optimisation.
You are looking for a research role. This is applied engineering; shipping products.
You prefer working in a well-defined system with clear tickets and sprint ceremonies. We're running like an R&D team, not a feature shipping team. Our practices are built on hypothesis testing and experiments, not sprints and story points.

WHY CATAPULT? 

We have amazing people. We promise you’ll work with some of the most ambitious,  intelligent people in an exciting industry, and do some of the best work of your life.
We encourage our people to engage in constructive, open, and honest communication to make Catapult extraordinary. 
We work in a collaborative yet challenging environment to consistently improve our performance, which in turn impacts our customers' performance.
Our workforce spans more than 20 countries. You'll have the opportunity to work with people from multiple nationalities and cultures, and to build your global awareness.  
We value improvement and development. We are challenging ourselves to continuously grow and become a high-performance company. That means we maintain a growth mindset in everything we do, and our people are always looking for ways to improve.  There is an unlimited opportunity to grow, do more, and do better.

Whether you’re interested in sports or not, you’ll have the satisfaction of knowing your work is supporting some of the most successful teams and athletes on the planet! 

Research shows that while men apply for jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalized groups tend only to apply when they check every box. So if you have what it takes, but don't meet every single point in our job ad, please still get in touch! We would love to have a chat and see if you could be a great addition to our team. We are building the future of sports performance. Our priority is to find the brightest talent who can add to our team culture, actively contribute, and be excited about what they do.

All offers of employment are subject to Catapult's positive prehire check. To find out more, please contact the Talent Partner for this role.

SEQ-JOB: Job description for Senior Engineer (FEA / CFD / ML)


Company Overview:

We are a leading engineering solutions provider in Australia and New Zealand, specialising in Finite Element Analysis (FEA), Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) modelling and Fitness for Service (FFS) assessments for Power Generation, Petrochemical, and Industrial manufacturing facilities. Our innovative approach to problem-solving, combined with cutting-edge simulation techniques, helps our clients optimise designs, improve performance, and evaluate the reliability of their products and processes.

Position Overview:

We are seeking a talented and experienced senior engineering analyst with expertise in FEA and/or CFD modelling to join our growing and dynamic team. The ideal candidate will play a crucial role in delivering high-quality simulation and analysis services to our clients across various industries, contributing to the success of their projects and driving innovation. Exposure and expertise in machine learning (ML) would also be beneficial.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead and execute FEA and CFD simulations for a wide array of industries, including but not limited to energy, oil & gas, aerospace, marine, automotive, manufacturing, and marine.

Collaborate closely with clients to understand their engineering challenges, provide expert guidance, and develop simulation strategies that address their needs.

Analyse and interpret simulation data to provide actionable recommendations for design improvements, performance enhancements, and risk mitigation.

Develop and implement advanced simulation techniques, methodologies, and best practices to ensure the accuracy and efficiency of analysis processes.

Prepare clear, professional technical reports and present simulation findings and engineering recommendations confidently to clients and stakeholders.

Stay up to date with industry trends, emerging technologies, and software advancements in the field of numerical simulation.

Qualifications:

Master's or Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering, Engineering Science, Aerospace, or a related field

Minimum of 5 years of hands-on experience in performing FEA and or CFD simulations

Proficiency in using industry-standard software tools such as STAR-CCM+, Abaqus, ANSYS or Nastran

Strong knowledge of solid mechanics, fluid dynamics, heat transfer, structural mechanics, and numerical methods

Knowledge of ASME FFS-1/API-579-1 and ASME-VIII-Div II Fitness for Service and pressure equipment codes

Demonstrated ability to analyse, interpret simulation data and communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Experience with accurate prediction of component life and structural margins, including creep, fatigue and fracture mechanics

Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to approach complex engineering challenges with creativity

Effective written and verbal communication

Benefits:

Competitive salary and flexible working hours

Opportunity to work on diverse and challenging projects across various industries

Collaborative and inclusive work environment that values innovation and expertise

Professional development opportunities and ongoing training in the latest simulation techniques and tools

Flexible work arrangements to achieve a healthy work-life balance

Access to cutting-edge technology and software tools

Contribution to meaningful projects that drive innovation

If you are a passionate and experienced engineer with a deep understanding of CFD, FEA modelling, and Machine Learning (ML), and if you thrive on delivering impactful solutions, we invite you to join our team and make a significant contribution to the success of our clients and the growth of our company. Apply now and be part of our exciting journey!

Job Description


To help us scale the AI systems behind that intelligence, we're looking for a Senior MLOps Engineer to join our  Platform team in Sydney.

This is a genuine opportunity to own greenfield platform work at serious scale. Nearmap is one of the top three AWS users in the APAC region. The systems you'll build aren't theoretical — they're processing aerial imagery across Australia, the US, and Canada, and serving AI models to real customers at high concurrency.

You'll sit between traditional DevOps and the new world of AI engineering — which means this role suits someone with a strong back-end and platform engineering foundation who is ready to grow their career into AI, or who is already making that transition. AI experience is a bonus, not a barrier to entry — we can coach that side as a team.

🔹 What you'll be doing

You'll own projects end to end — from architecture and design through to implementation, delivery, and production operations. That kind of trust and autonomy is real here, not a recruitment line.

Day to day that looks like:

Designing and building scalable ML infrastructure — large-scale batch inference on EKS, real-time model serving via Ray Serve, and GPU-accelerated distributed training on GCP and AWS
Building the LLM platform that will underpin Nearmap's next generation of generative AI products — including deployment, governance, experimentation, and cost optimisation across CPU/GPU workloads
Owning developer experience for AICV engineering teams — CI/CD pipelines, Docker tooling, ArgoCD, W&B, and Arize AI
Driving reliability and observability across production ML systems with real SLAs and SLOs
Contributing production-quality Python to shared platform libraries and services
Playing a tech lead role on your primary project, with advisory exposure across others
Engaging directly with ML tooling vendors including AWS, GCP, Weights & Biases, Confluent, and Arize AI

The teams depending on your platform include Model R&D, Computer Vision, Insurance Data Science, Agentic AI, and AI Map Data.

Qualifications


What we're looking for

Must-haves:

Production experience building and operating Kubernetes-based platforms (EKS/GKE) at scale
High-scale, high-concurrency API development and operation in production environments
Real operational mindset: you've managed systems with SLAs and SLOs and stayed close through incidents
Strong DevOps fundamentals — CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, deployment pipelines
Production-level Python — services and libraries, not just scripts

Highly valued:

Ray Serve or equivalent real-time model serving experience
LLM or generative AI platform work in production (beyond PoC)
ML observability tooling — Arize AI, Evidently, Prometheus/Grafana
Infrastructure-as-code with Terraform
Experience at terabyte or petabyte scale
GPU/CPU cost optimisation

A degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or a related field is welcome — equivalent practical experience is equally valued.

This role is based in our Barangaroo, Sydney office, with an expectation of at least two days per week on-site.

This role is open to Australian citizens and permanent residents, or those with full working rights in Australia.

Additional Information


Some of our benefits

Nearmap takes a holistic approach to our employees’ emotional, physical and financial wellness. Some of our current benefits include:

Quarterly wellbeing day off - Four additional days off annually for your 'YOU' Days
Wellbeing and technology allowance
Annual flu vaccinations
Hybrid flexibility for this role 
Nearmap subscription (of course!)
Stocked kitchen with access to all the snacks you need
In-office lunch every Tuesday and Thursday at our Sydney CBD office
Showers available for anyone cycling to work or lunchtime gym-goers!

Working at Nearmap

We move fast, we care about craft, and we're proud of what we're building. If you're energised by turning hard problems into real-world impact, we'd love to meet you.


If you can see yourself working at Nearmap and feel you have the right level of experience, we invite you to get in touch. 

Read the product documentation for Nearmap AI:

https://docs.nearmap.com/display/ND/NEARMAP+AI

For a deep dive into Nearmap AI, listen to AI Systems Senior Director Mike Bewley on the Mapscaping podcast https://mapscaping.com/blogs/the-mapscaping-podcast/collecting-and-processing-aerial-imagery-at-scale

Thanks, but we got this! Nearmap does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruitment agencies and search firms. Please do not email or send unsolicited resumes to any Nearmap employee, location or address. Nearmap is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Role Overview
An opportunity exists for an experienced Onsite Support Engineer to join a growing Service Team supporting a range of external clients across the ACT region.

This role sits at the L2–L3 level, focused on delivering hands-on, face-to-face technical support, owning escalations, and resolving complex site-based issues across infrastructure, networking, and cloud environments.

You’ll play a key role in maintaining service quality, driving SLA outcomes, and acting as a trusted technical point of contact for clients.

This is a hands-on position combining onsite client support with broader service operations and escalation responsibilities when not in the field.
What you’ll be doing
Provide onsite L2–L3 technical support across infrastructure, network, and application environments
Act as a key escalation point for complex client site issues, taking ownership through to resolution
Perform advanced troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and problem management across recurring incidents
Diagnose and resolve incidents and service requests within agreed SLAs, including high-priority and business-critical issues
Lead and coordinate onsite incident resolution, including working with internal teams and vendors where required
Provide clear, confident communication to clients, including technical explanations and post-incident summaries
Attend scheduled client sites and proactively assess environment health, risks, and improvement opportunities
Escalate only where necessary, providing full context and continuing ownership of the issue
Accurately document technical findings, resolutions, and environment changes in ticketing systems
Support service operations when not onsite, including handling escalations, complex tickets, and backlog reduction
Identify and drive improvements in service delivery, recurring issues, and client environments
Mentor and support junior engineers, providing guidance on troubleshooting and best practices
Ensure adherence to security standards, compliance requirements, and client-specific policies
Participate in an on-call roster where applicable
What we’re looking for
3–5+ years’ experience in an MSP or onsite IT support environment
Strong capability across L2–L3 infrastructure, networking, and systems support
Proven experience handling escalations and complex technical issues end-to-end
Strong troubleshooting skills across Windows environments, networking, and cloud platforms
Experience with enterprise environments (Active Directory, Microsoft 365, Azure, etc.)
Confidence working directly with clients and acting as a technical escalation point
Ability to prioritise, manage, and own multiple complex issues in a fast-paced environment
Comfortable operating with autonomy while knowing when to escalate appropriately
Desirable experience
Networking technologies (Cisco, Fortinet, Aruba, Ubiquiti, WatchGuard)
Microsoft ecosystem (Windows Server, Active Directory, Microsoft 365, Azure)
Endpoint and security tools (Defender, Sophos, MDM solutions, vulnerability tools)
Voice/telephony systems (Teams Voice, SIP, 3CX)
Virtualisation and backup environments (VMware, Veeam, Nutanix, Synology)
ITIL awareness or certification
Why this role?
This is a strong opportunity for someone who enjoys being out in the field, working directly with clients, and solving technical problems hands-on.

The role offers exposure across a wide range of technologies and environments, with the ability to grow within a service team that is scaling onsite support capability across the region.
  
#SCR-ryley-ladhams

Join the change. Electrify your future!

We've been proudly Aussie since 1837, always finding new ways to innovate in energy and essential services. Now, we're moving towards a sustainable future through electrification and investing in renewable energy-and we'd like you to join us. Whether you're on-site, in the office, or somewhere in between, you'll find opportunities to grow your career here. You'll work with a team that's passionate about powering Australian lives, in a culture that values inclusivity, respect, and learning. Help us create a cleaner energy future, and we'll back you every step of the way as you build your career.


About the role

Reporting to the Asset Data & Governance Manager, we are seeking an experienced Data Engineer who is ready to step up as a technical lead and trusted advisor. This role is critical in driving the convergence of Operational Technology (OT) and Information Technology (IT) through advanced data engineering.

Working closely with Analytics, OT, and IT teams, you'll deliver reliable and efficient data integration, empowering operational business units to achieve excellence and unlock new efficiencies.


Key responsibilities

Design, develop and maintain secure, reliable data pipelines and transformation processes to ensure high data quality, consistency and availability.
Build and support API‑based data integrations, including ingestion, synchronisation and validation of data from CMMS platforms (e.g., MaintainX) and other BESS‑related systems.
Manage and optimise data storage and retrieval across operational systems, enterprise platforms and central data repositories, ensuring performance and scalability.
Document end‑to‑end data flows, integration points, dependencies and lineage in a clear, structured and maintainable manner.
Develop reusable scripts, tools and automation to uplift data quality, improve accessibility and support analytics, modelling and visualisation activities.


About you

You're a collaborative data leader who helps teams embrace data integration and interoperability, and champions continuous improvement to deliver reliable, high-quality data and measurable business outcomes. Additionally, you will have most of the following:

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Information Systems, Software Engineering, Data Engineering, or equivalent professional experience.
Strong skills in Python and SQL for data engineering tasks; familiarity with languages like Scala or Java for big data integration.
Hands-on experience with data pipelines, ETL/ELT, delta sharing processes, and integration of operational or time-series data.
Solid understanding of DevOps practices and tools (e.g., Git, CI/CD, automated testing).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to identify issues and implement lasting solutions.
Excellent communication and documentation skills, including technical diagrams and system descriptions.
Working knowledge of the energy sector and the convergence of Operational Technology (OT) and Information Technology (IT).



Inclusion at AGL

At AGL, we value diversity and welcome applicants from various backgrounds, including Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander peoples, individuals with disabilities, culturally diverse individuals, and members of the LGBTQ+ community. AGL is dedicated to maintaining a diverse workforce and encourages applicants to share their lived experiences.
We offer reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process and respect the privacy of those who choose not to disclose. To learn more visit: www.agl.com.au/careers

AGL is proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer for All Women.

Our pre-employment screening process includes a Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Check and a medical assessment if necessary.
The information provided as part of your application to AGL will be managed in accordance with the AGL Privacy Policy.
AGL does not accept agency submissions unless approval has been granted by a member of the Talent Acquisition team. Candidates submitted by an agency without approval will not be considered by AGL.

Shape the future of AI Platforms

We're looking for a Senior AI Platform Engineer to join a high-performing engineering team focused on building scalable, secure, and modern AI platforms in the cloud.

This is an opportunity to work on enterprise-scale AI and cloud initiatives, helping define platform architecture, automation standards, governance, and AI-first engineering practices across a rapidly evolving environment.

You'll collaborate closely with cloud engineers, architects, and development teams to deliver reusable platform capabilities that accelerate innovation and improve operational efficiency.

What you'll be doing

Designing and building scalable AI platform solutions in AWS
Developing secure, cloud-native architectures and automation frameworks
Embedding AI capabilities into engineering workflows and core systems
Driving platform governance, operational excellence, and cost optimisation
Creating reusable patterns and improving engineering maturity across teams
Mentoring engineers and promoting modern engineering best practices

What you'll bring

Strong experience across AWS cloud services, infrastructure, networking, and security
Hands-on experience delivering AI/ML solutions in cloud environments
Advanced Python development skills and API integration experience
Experience designing scalable and secure cloud architectures
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Passion for mentoring and uplifting engineering capability

What's on offer

Hybrid working environment
Additional annual leave
Generous parental leave program
Learning and development support
Collaborative and innovation-driven culture
Wellness initiatives, team events, and onsite perks
Long-term career growth opportunities

If you're passionate about AI platforms, cloud engineering, and building modern scalable solutions, apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Ad Text:
Join an Award-Winning Team — We're Hiring an MSP Automation Engineer!

RES. Business IT is a leading provider of IT Managed Services, cloud, and communications solutions across Australia. We're proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work, a recognition of our commitment to building an outstanding culture where our people thrive.

If you're someone who looks at a manual process and immediately thinks "this should be automated", we'd love to hear from you.




About Us

RES. Business IT exists to Enable the Success of Others by delivering Technology that Simplifies the Complex.

We are a leading end-to-end IT provider delivering responsible, efficient, and intelligent solutions across advisory, deployment, and management, tailored to our clients' needs and strategically built for the future.

Automation is not new to us. We have a working automation environment already delivering real operational value across the business, and we are now investing in dedicated capability to take it to the next level. This is your opportunity to own that function.




About the Role

This is a newly created, hands-on role where you will work closely with the leadership team and across every part of the business including Managed Services, Consulting Services, Sales, Finance, and Operations.

This is not a BAU role. You will have real ownership of the automation function at RES, with the scope to make a genuine impact across the entire business.

You will inherit a functioning automation environment built across platforms including Rewst, Azure Logic Apps, Power Automate, Azure Functions, and ConnectWise PSA. Your job is to grow it in a structured, scalable, and well-governed way and to keep finding the next thing worth building.

We are also actively exploring how AI can enhance our automation capability, and we expect this person to have a view on where and how it fits.

We also expect you to suggest things we haven't thought of yet. That's part of the role.




What You Will Be Working On

Designing and delivering automations that reduce manual effort and improve consistency across all business functions

Refining and improving existing workflows such as customer monthly reporting and ticket triage

Identifying automation opportunities across the business and developing straightforward business cases with ROI estimates

Maintaining the health and reliability of the automation platform stack and its integrations

Documenting all automations in IT Glue and establishing governance standards across the environment

Working alongside teams to build automation confidence and capability across RES




About You

3 to 5 years experience in automation, systems integration, or MSP operations

Strong, demonstrable experience with ConnectWise PSA including workflows, ticket routing, and API usage — this is critical to what we do

Hands-on experience with Rewst, Azure Logic Apps, Power Automate, or Azure Functions

Strong working knowledge of Microsoft 365 including Entra ID, Exchange Online, SharePoint, Teams, and Intune

Proficiency in PowerShell with experience in other scripting languages advantageous

Experience with REST API and webhook integrations across multiple vendor platforms

An understanding of where AI tools add genuine value in an automation context, and the judgement to know when to use them and when not to

Microsoft certifications highly regarded

Self-directed, commercially minded, and able to manage competing priorities without close supervision

Prior MSP experience is highly desirable

Permanent Australian work rights required




What We Offer

Flexible working arrangements with the ability to work from home (Brisbane area)

Paid study resources and exams, with support for certifications relevant to your role

Recognition programs including service anniversaries and monthly awards

InstaPay (Earned Wage Access) and salary sacrifice of IT equipment

Access to a discount marketplace with savings at retailers including Uber Eats, JB Hi-Fi, Dan Murphy's and more

An Employee Assistance Program to support your social and psychological wellbeing

A collaborative and inclusive culture with direct access to an approachable leadership team

Monthly leadership updates and regular social celebrations

Certified as a Great Place to Work




Ready to Apply?

We are an MSP with automation already in place and big plans to take it further. Before you apply, take a look at what we do at resit.com.au and have a think about what you would build first and why.

When you apply, skip the cover letter template. Send us a short note with your answer to that question instead. It tells us more about how you think than any CV ever could.

No contact from agencies please.
